description: A designed single-subject 'DEPORTED' card in ICE Denver's standard template: medium-blue background with faint topographic-line texture. The centered square photograph is a front-facing booking/intake portrait of a man with short dark hair and a thin mustache wearing a dark shirt, photographed inside an institutional room (window and fixtures visible behind him). A bold red banner across the bottom of the photo reads 'DEPORTED' in large white all-caps. Below, large white serif type gives the name 'Rigoberto Carranza-Mendez', followed by smaller white text 'CONVICTED OF MURDER AND DUI AND HAS BEEN REMOVED FROM THE UNITED STATES.' ('MURDER AND DUI' bolded). Beneath that, spaced-out faint capitals read 'DENVER', and the DHS seal with the 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' wordmark appears at the bottom. The technique is a completed-action 'trophy' card celebrating a removal, paired with a tweet mocking 'sanctuary' Denver. Target audience: general public and supporters of aggressive deportation policy.
notable_text: DEPORTED; Rigoberto Carranza-Mendez; CONVICTED OF MURDER AND DUI AND HAS BEEN REMOVED FROM THE UNITED STATES; DENVER
